summary: Code-path review confirmed the diff leaves one core legacy visibility gap unresolved and introduces two state-management edge cases in the new UI helpers.

findings.title: Execution-ready visibility still trusts summary fields over DB truth
findings.detail: The list query never batch-loads tasks by planningRequestId, and the detail model resolves the top-level problem summary before it knows how many derivedTasks were actually found. That means legacy execution-ready rows can show zero tasks / 'no visible task records' while the derived task section below is populated from real Task rows.
findings.evidence: buildPlanningRequestDetailViews() builds derivedTasks after problem: resolvePlanningRequestProblemSummary(request), and getPlanningRequestsByProjectId() performs no task enrichment at all.
findings.trigger_condition: legacy rows with missing handoff summary or stale derivedTaskCount
findings.potential_consequence: The new visibility feature contradicts itself across summary and detail surfaces, especially for migrated data.

findings.title: Retrying analysis can be flagged stalled immediately
findings.detail: Because the stall check uses createdAt instead of a timestamp tied to the active analysis attempt, any retried request older than five minutes becomes '分析尚未推进' as soon as it returns to analyzing.
findings.evidence: hasPlanningAnalysisStalled(createdAt) compares Date.parse(createdAt) directly against Date.now().
findings.potential_consequence: The problem-stage helper produces misleading warnings and suggested actions.

findings.title: New handoff dialog reintroduces the stale-response race the detail sheet already guarded against
findings.detail: The detail panel got token-based stale response protection, but the new handoff preview path did not. The result is the exact close/switch race that Story 3.5 called out as something to regression-test and avoid in request-linked detail flows.
findings.evidence: detailRequestTokenRef / shouldIgnorePlanningDetailResponse exist for detail loading, but no equivalent exists for getPlanningRequestHandoffPreviewAction().
findings.trigger_condition: parallel or quickly sequenced preview opens
findings.potential_consequence: The dialog can present stale candidates and deferred selections from the wrong planning request.
